title: Exploit next-row PitStop consistency as a data construction probe
summary: Because the target is PitNextLap and both train/test include current-lap PitStop, the next row for the same Year/Race/Driver sequence may directly reveal the label for all non-terminal laps.
rationale: Because the target is PitNextLap and both train/test include current-lap PitStop, the next row for the same Year/Race/Driver sequence may directly reveal the label for all non-terminal laps. This is a validation trap worth testing explicitly before adding more weak features.
implementation_hint: Sort by Year, Race, Driver, LapNumber, then create NextObservedPitStop = groupby(...).PitStop.shift(-1). Use it either as a feature or as a rule-based override where the next lap exists; fall back to the current best model for terminal/missing sequence rows.
expected_effect: Could produce a step-change in AUC if the dataset contains complete future telemetry; otherwise it will quickly expose whether local CV has been inflated by row-order leakage.
risk: May be considered leakage if the intended prediction setting forbids using future test rows; public LB will decide whether Kaggle’s provided test table permits it.
